zh.lifeSummary: 當Marred Ferch Daffydd aka Margaret Hanmer於1357年出生在英國威爾斯福林爾馬洛 薩斯格時，她的父親Sir David Hanmer29歲，母親Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu25歲。她和Owain Glyndŵr ap Gruffydd至少有5個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於1420年在英國英格蘭賀福爾享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Hanmer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: habitational name from Hanmer (Flintshire), named with the Old English personal name Hagena + Old English mere ‘lake, pond’. A daughter of Sir David Hanmer, a Welsh judge in the 14th century, was married to the Welsh ruler Owen Glendower, who led the revolt against Henry IV's rule in Wales.
English: habitational name from Anmer (Norfolk), from Old English æned ‘duck’ + mere ‘lake, pond’.

nameMeanings.name: Marred
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Daffydd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of David . In widespread use during the Middle Ages, it was later largely replaced by the English form, but since the late 19th century it has come into its own again.

nameMeanings.name: aka
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by one of the early kings of Judah, who reigned for forty years, as recorded in 1 Kings and 2 Chronicles. It was originally a byname meaning ‘doctor, healer’ in Hebrew, and is still a common Jewish name. It was first used among English-speaking Christians by the Puritans in the 17th century.

variants.name: Åse

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. en.lifeSummary: When Marred Ferch Daffydd aka Margaret Hanmer was born in 1357, in Maelor Saesneg, Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Sir David Hanmer, was 29 and her mother, Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu, was 25. She had at least 5 sons and 5 daughters with Owain Glyndŵr ap Gruffydd. She died in 1420, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marred Ferch Daffydd aka Margaret Hanmer est née en 1357, à Maelor Saesneg, Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir David Hanmer, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu, avait 25 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles avec Owain Glyndŵr ap Gruffydd. Elle est décédée en 1420, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hatte mit Owain Glyndŵr ap Gruffydd mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1420 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Marred Ferch Daffydd aka Margaret Hanmerが1357年にイギリスウェールズフリントシュアイアメイラー サエスネグで生まれた時，父Sir David Hanmerは29歳，母Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Dduは25歳であった。彼女はOwain Glyndŵr ap Gruffyddとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1420年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Marred Ferch Daffydd aka Margaret Hanmer(이)가 1357년 영국 웨일스 플린츠히르 메이로러 사이스네그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir David Hanmer(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 Owain Glyndŵr ap Gruffydd(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marred Ferch Daffydd aka Margaret Hanmer родилась в 1357 г. в местности мэелор саеснег, флинцхир, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir David Hanmer было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu, 25. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей от Owain Glyndŵr ap Gruffydd. Она умерла в 1420 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marred Ferch Daffydd aka Margaret Hanmer nació en 1357, en Maelor Saesneg, Flintshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir David Hanmer, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas con Owain Glyndŵr ap Gruffydd. Murió en 1420, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultada en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
